steps.title: Set up the equation for a 6% increase
steps.content: When n is increased by 6%, the new value is $1.06n$. We're told this equals 318: $$1.06n = 318$$

steps.title: Test choice C
steps.content: Backsolving — testing choice C (300): If $n = 300$, then $$1.06 \times 300 = 300 + 0.06 \times 300 = 300 + 18 = 318$$ That confirms C is correct.
